subtitle: 29 days with the prophets before him ﷺ

source.note: Mufti Ismail Menk's beloved Stories of the Prophets series, 29 episodes, from the creation of Adam to Isa. Retold faithfully in the brand voice and credited to him, never reproduced verbatim.

phases.title: Why their stories
phases.blurb: Why Allah fills the Qur'an with the prophets before Him ﷺ, and how to read their lives

phases.title: The first human beings
phases.blurb: Adam, the father of us all, his sons, and the first souls raised to Allah

phases.title: The early warners
phases.blurb: Nuh, Hud, and Salih, sent to nations that mistook their strength for safety

phases.title: The friend of the Most Merciful
phases.blurb: Ibrahim and Ismail, the broken idols, the fire that cooled, and the Ka'bah raised

phases.title: Tested, then chosen
phases.blurb: Yaqub's patience, Yusuf's well and throne, and the trials of Ayyub and Yunus

phases.title: Musa and the Children of Israel
phases.blurb: The staff, the sea, the Pharaoh, the law, and Shu'ayb's honest scales

phases.title: The kings who bowed
phases.blurb: Dawud and Sulayman, given a kingdom and kept humble, and the line of Zakariyya

phases.title: The word of Allah
phases.blurb: Isa, his mother Maryam, and the promise of the messenger to come after him ﷺ

note: One episode per day, 29 days. Each entry is retold faithfully in the brand voice from Mufti Menk's lecture, credited to him, never reproduced verbatim (the same way the Companions pillar retells Omar Suleiman and the Seerah retells Yasir Qadhi). The Qur'an is grounded via quran.ai, never from memory. Where Mufti Menk relays an Israiliyyat report or weighs an account, his caveat is kept. Every entry bridges back into the wider library so the prophet's life connects to the Qur'an, the Seerah, and the reader's own faith.
